task_completion.description: Open the 'blobs.gif' sample image in Fiji
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Fiji application window is open and displaying the 'blobs.gif' image (a grayscale image of various blob shapes).

task_completion.description: Convert the image to single-pixel outlines of the objects
task_completion.visual_evidence: The image in Fiji is transformed into a binary image showing only the thin, single-pixel borders/outlines of the blobs (typically black outlines on a white background or vice versa).

task_completion.description: Perform the Fractal Box Count analysis
task_completion.visual_evidence: A Fiji results table or a plot window titled 'Fractal Box Count' (or similar) is visible, showing the calculated Fractal Dimension (D).

task_completion.description: Save the resulting data table to the specified CSV path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, terminal command, or file manager showing the results table being saved to '~/Fiji_Data/results/fractal/fractal_results.csv'.

task_completion.description: Save the log-log box count plot to the specified PNG path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, terminal command, or file manager showing the log-log plot being saved to '~/Fiji_Data/results/fractal/fractal_plot.png'.

integrity.description: The agent used the Fiji graphical user interface to perform the analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Fiji's menus (e.g., Process > Binary > Outline, Analyze > Tools > Fractal Box Count) rather than just running a headless script in the terminal.

integrity.description: The generated plot is a genuine Fiji output
integrity.visual_evidence: The plot window matches Fiji's native plotting style (simple axes, specific default fonts, 'y = a + b*x' fit equation displayed) rather than a generic Python/matplotlib plot.

integrity.description: The agent did not mock the output files using text editors or echo commands
integrity.visual_evidence: The CSV and PNG files are generated by exporting from Fiji, not by typing expected values (like D=1.15 or D=1.39) directly into a terminal or text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected fractal dimension (D) for the outlines of blobs.gif is approximately 1.39 (typically ranging from 1.34 to 1.65 depending on the exact threshold used). The standard Fiji box counting algorithm uses box sizes [2, 3, 4, 6, 8, 12, 16, 32, 64]. The expected output paths are /home/ga/Fiji_Data/results/fractal/fractal_results.csv and /home/ga/Fiji_Data/results/fractal/fractal_plot.png.
